chapter.label: Tale 116
chapter.title: The Brother Who Was Falsely Accused Of Taking A Piece Of Gold

paragraphs.text: ‘When I was a young man, my abba and 1 withdrew from Raithou and came to Palestine where we stayed wilh an elder. The elder who was our host possessed one piece of gold; but he forgot where he

had put it and began Lo suspect that I, young man that I was, had stolen it. The elder said to the fathers of the place: ‘Brother Andrew took the piece of gold". My abba heard this. He called me and said: “Tell me, Brother Andrew, have you taken the elder’s piece of gold?

I said: ‘Spare me, abba; I took nothing’. I had a cloak however, so 1 sold it for one picce of gold. I took the coin, went to the elder and prostrated myself’ before him, saying: ‘Abba, forgive me, sic. Satan led me astray and I took your piece of gold’. There was a worldling there and the elder said: ‘Go away child; I rave lost nothing’. Again 1 prostrated myself before him and said: ‘For the Lord's sake, take the piece of gold (here, this is it) and pray for me; because Satan deceived me into stealing and causing you trouble’. The elder said: ‘Child, I lost nothing at ali’. Because he could not convince me, the worldling said to me: ‘Of a truth, brother, when I came yesterday, sir, I (ound the elder in tears, prostrating himself in great affliction.
Whea I saw him in so great tribulation, I said to him: “Of your charity, tell me what is matter with you". He said to me: “I wrongly i accused the brother of taking my piece of gold aad, look! I have
found it where I put it”. Then the ckler was encouraged by the fact
that, although I had not taken the piece of gold, [ still brought it
back to him, saying: “Take you piece of gold, for it was I who took
